# A Boundary Case in a Static Signaling Model

## 1. Setup

An agent chooses action $a \in \{0,1\}$. Action 1 yields payoff $\theta-c$ and action 0 yields zero. The parameter $\theta$ is public, and $c>0$.

## 2. Result

The agent chooses action 1 when $\theta>c$ and action 0 when $\theta<c$. At $\theta=c$, both actions maximize payoff.

## 3. Proposition

The equilibrium action is unique for every parameter value.

The proposition characterize the model's comparative static.
